# Pyreon Framework

> Signal-based UI framework for TypeScript. SSR, SSG, islands, SPA. Standard JSX + TypeScript.
> Top-tier performance on the JS Framework Benchmark ‚Äî competitive with Solid (most rows tied within noise), ahead of Vue and React.

## Signals (reactivity)

```typescript
import { signal, computed, effect, batch } from "@pyreon/reactivity"

const count = signal(0)       // create
count()                        // read (subscribes)
count.set(5)                   // write
count.update(n => n + 1)       // update
count.peek()                   // read without subscribing

const doubled = computed(() => count() * 2)  // auto-tracks deps
effect(() => console.log(count()))           // runs when count changes
batch(() => { a.set(1); b.set(2) })          // single notification
```

## Components

```typescript
// Components are plain functions that run ONCE
const Counter = (props: { initial: number }) => {
  const count = signal(props.initial)
  return <button onClick={() => count.update(n => n + 1)}>{count()}</button>
}
```

## Conditional Rendering

```typescript
import { Show, Switch, Match } from "@pyreon/core"

<Show when={isLoggedIn()} fallback={<Login />}>
  <Dashboard />
</Show>
```

## List Rendering

```typescript
import { For } from "@pyreon/core"

// Use "by" not "key" (JSX reserves "key" for VNode reconciliation)
<For each={items()} by={item => item.id}>
  {item => <li>{item.name}</li>}
</For>
```

## Lifecycle

```typescript
import { onMount, onUnmount, provide } from "@pyreon/core"
import { onCleanup } from "@pyreon/reactivity"

onMount(() => {
  const id = setInterval(tick, 1000)
  return () => clearInterval(id)  // optional cleanup
})
```

## Context

```typescript
import { createContext, useContext, provide } from "@pyreon/core"

const Theme = createContext<"light" | "dark">("light")

// Provide (auto-cleans up on unmount):
provide(Theme, "dark")

// Consume:
const theme = useContext(Theme)
```

## Smart `class` with `cx()`

```typescript
import { cx } from "@pyreon/core"

// Strings, arrays, objects, nested mix ‚Äî all work:
<div class="base active" />
<div class={["base", isActive() && "active"]} />
<div class={{ base: true, active: isActive() }} />
<div class={cx("base", isActive() && "active", { bold: isBold() })} />
```

## Props Utilities

```typescript
import { splitProps, mergeProps, createUniqueId } from "@pyreon/core"

// Split props preserving reactivity:
const [local, rest] = splitProps(props, ["class", "style"])

// Merge default props:
const merged = mergeProps({ size: "md", variant: "primary" }, props)

// SSR-safe unique IDs:
const id = createUniqueId()  // "pyreon-1", "pyreon-2", etc.
```

## Typed Events

```typescript
// TargetedEvent types currentTarget per element ‚Äî no casts needed:
<input onInput={(e: TargetedEvent<HTMLInputElement>) => {
  console.log(e.currentTarget.value)  // typed!
}} />
```

## Effect Cleanup

```typescript
import { effect, onCleanup } from "@pyreon/reactivity"

effect(() => {
  const handler = () => console.log(count())
  window.addEventListener("resize", handler)
  onCleanup(() => window.removeEventListener("resize", handler))
})
```

## Routing

```typescript
import { createRouter, RouterProvider, RouterView, RouterLink, useRouter, useRoute, useTypedSearchParams, useTransition, useMiddlewareData } from "@pyreon/router"

const router = createRouter([
  { path: "/", component: Home },
  { path: "/user/:id", component: User, loader: ({ params }) => fetchUser(params.id) },
])

<RouterProvider router={router}>
  <RouterLink to="/">Home</RouterLink>
  <RouterView />
</RouterProvider>

// Navigation:
const router = useRouter()
router.push("/settings")
router.back()
```

## Head Management

```typescript
import { useHead } from "@pyreon/head"

useHead({ title: "Page Title", meta: [{ name: "description", content: "..." }] })
useHead(() => ({ title: `${name()} ‚Äî Profile` }))  // reactive
```

## SSR

```typescript
import { createHandler } from "@pyreon/server"

export default createHandler({ App, routes, mode: "stream" })
```

## Islands (Partial Hydration)

```typescript
import { island } from "@pyreon/server"

const SearchBar = island(() => import("./SearchBar"), { name: "SearchBar", hydrate: "visible" })
```

## Mount (Client)

```typescript
import { mount } from "@pyreon/runtime-dom"

mount(<App />, document.getElementById("app")!)
```

## Common Mistakes ‚Äî NEVER DO THIS

### Signals
- `{count}` ‚Üí `{count()}` ‚Äî signals are functions, call them to read
- `signal(5)` ‚Üí `signal.set(5)` ‚Äî calling signal with args does NOT write (dev mode warns)
- `.value` ‚Üí signals are callable, not `.value` getters (that's Vue)
- `useState` ‚Üí `signal()`, `useEffect` ‚Üí `effect()` or `onMount()`
- `memo(Component)` ‚Üí not needed, components run once in Pyreon

### JSX
- `key={...}` on `<For>` ‚Üí use `by={...}` prop (`key` is reserved for VNode reconciliation)
- `className` ‚Üí `class`, `htmlFor` ‚Üí `for` (standard HTML attributes)
- `onChange` on `<input>` ‚Üí `onInput` for keypress-by-keypress (native DOM events)
- `.map()` in JSX ‚Üí use `<For>` for reactive list rendering
- `class="a b"` works, but for conditional classes use `class={["a", cond && "b"]}` or `cx()`

### Props & Reactivity
- `const { state } = props` ‚Üí BREAKS REACTIVITY! Destructuring captures once. Use `props.state` for reactive access
- `const x = props.y` in JSX is NOW REACTIVE ‚Äî compiler inlines `props.y` at JSX use sites (text + attributes). Transitive: `const a = props.x; const b = a + 1` inlines as `((props.x) + 1)`. Only `const` tracked (`let`/`var` excluded). Non-JSX usage stays static.
- For rest props with reactivity: `const [own, rest] = splitProps(props, ['state', 'size'])` ‚Äî preserves getters
- Component props with signal reads ARE reactive (compiler wraps with _rp): `<Button state={isActive() ? 'primary' : 'default'} />` updates without remount
- Single JSX element props are stable: `<Wrapper icon={<Icon name={x()} />} />` ‚Äî Icon created once, name updates reactively
- Conditional JSX props remount: `<Wrapper icon={show() ? <Icon /> : null} />` ‚Äî correct, different VNode types

### Context & Effects
- `const { mode } = useContext(ctx)` ‚Üí BREAKS REACTIVITY! Destructuring captures once. Keep reference: `const ctx = useContext(Ctx)` then `ctx.mode`
- `effect(() => { effect(() => ...) })` ‚Üí NEVER nest effects. Use `computed()` for derived values
- `.peek()` inside effects/computeds ‚Üí bypasses tracking, creates stale reads

### Providers
- Use `<PyreonUI theme={theme} mode="light">` ‚Äî this is the ONLY provider you need
- Do NOT use `UnistyleProvider`, `ThemeProvider`, or `CoreProvider` directly ‚Äî they are internal

### Other
- `(e.target as HTMLInputElement)` ‚Üí use `TargetedEvent<HTMLInputElement>` for typed `currentTarget`
- `onMount(() => { doStuff() })` ‚Üí can optionally return a cleanup function
- `onClick={maybeUndefined}` ‚Üí safe (runtime bails), but be aware the handler is checked at assignment time

## Core Framework (6 packages)

- @pyreon/reactivity ‚Äî Fine-grained reactivity: signal, computed, effect, batch, onCleanup, createStore, watch, createResource, untrack. Pyreon signals are NOT `.value` getters (Vue ref) or `[state, setState]` tuples (React useState). The signal IS the function: `count()` reads, `count.set(v)` writes, `count.update(fn)` derives. This is the #1 confusion for developers coming from other frameworks.
- @pyreon/core ‚Äî VNode, h(), Fragment, lifecycle, context, JSX runtime, Suspense, ErrorBoundary, lazy(), Dynamic, cx(), splitProps, mergeProps, createUniqueId. Pyreon components are plain functions that execute a single time. Reactivity comes from reading signals inside reactive scopes (JSX expression thunks, `effect()`, `computed()`), not from re-running the component function. `if (!cond()) return null` at the top level runs once and is static ‚Äî use `return (() => { if (!cond()) return null; return <div /> })` for reactive conditional rendering.
- @pyreon/runtime-dom ‚Äî DOM renderer, mount, hydrateRoot, Transition, TransitionGroup, KeepAlive, SVG/MathML namespace, custom elements. SVG and MathML elements ALWAYS use `setAttribute()` for prop forwarding, never property assignment. Many SVG properties (`markerWidth`, `refX`, etc.) are read-only `SVGAnimatedLength` getters ‚Äî `el[key] = value` crashes. Detected by `el.namespaceURI !== "http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml"`.
- @pyreon/router ‚Äî hash+history+SSR, context-based, prefetching, guards, loaders, useIsActive, View Transitions, middleware, typed search params. `await router.push()` resolves after `updateCallbackDone` (DOM commit), NOT after animation finishes. It does NOT wait for `.finished` (~200-300ms). `.ready` and `.finished` get empty `.catch()` handlers so `AbortError: Transition was skipped` rejections (from interrupted transitions) do not leak as unhandled promise rejections.
- @pyreon/head ‚Äî Reactive `<head>` tag management ‚Äî useHead(), HeadProvider, renderWithHead() for SSR. Tags with the same key replace each other (innermost wins). Meta keys: `name` ‚Üí `property` ‚Üí index. Link keys: `href + rel` ‚Üí `rel` ‚Üí index. Script keys: `src` ‚Üí index. Style and noscript are unkeyed and always accumulated.
- @pyreon/server ‚Äî SSR + SSG + island architecture ‚Äî createHandler(), prerender(), island(), middleware chain. `mode: "stream"` uses `renderToStream` with shell + app + tail emission; Suspense boundaries resolve out-of-order. The `<head>` collected via `@pyreon/head` is flushed in the shell before any Suspense boundaries resolve, so async-loaded data does not contribute to it.

## UI System (12 packages)

- @pyreon/ui-core ‚Äî Unified `PyreonUI` provider (theme + mode + config), `useMode()` hook, init() escape hatch. The legacy split (separate theme / mode / config providers) is removed. `PyreonUI` is the only correct mount; calling `init()` directly is the escape hatch for SSR or test environments where the provider tree is unavailable.
- @pyreon/styler ‚Äî CSS-in-JS: styled(), css, keyframes, theming
- @pyreon/unistyle ‚Äî Responsive breakpoints, CSS property mappings, unit utilities, theme enrichment. Every visual package (`elements`, `rocketstyle`, `coolgrid`, `kinetic`) reads breakpoints / spacing / unit conventions from this package. Override defaults via `enrichTheme()` once at the app root rather than per-component.
- @pyreon/hooks ‚Äî 34 signal-based hooks: state (useToggle/usePrevious/useLatest/useControllableState), DOM (useEventListener/useClickOutside/useFocus/useHover/useFocusTrap/useElementSize/useWindowResize/useScrollLock/useIntersection/useInfiniteScroll), responsive (useBreakpoint/useMediaQuery/useColorScheme/useReducedMotion/useThemeValue/useSpacing/useRootSize), timing (useDebouncedValue/useDebouncedCallback/useThrottledCallback/useInterval/useTimeout/useTimeAgo), interaction (useClipboard/useDialog/useKeyboard/useOnline), composition (useMergedRef/useUpdateEffect/useIsomorphicLayoutEffect). `useControllableState({ value, defaultValue, onChange })` is the canonical controlled/uncontrolled pattern. Every primitive in `@pyreon/ui-primitives` uses it. Reimplementing the `isControlled + signal + getter` shape by hand was the #1 anti-pattern across primitives before the helper landed. Pass `value` and `defaultValue` as FUNCTIONS so signal reads track reactively ‚Äî a plain value loses prop-driven controlled/uncontrolled detection.
- @pyreon/elements ‚Äî Foundational primitives (Element, Text, List, Overlay, Portal)
- @pyreon/attrs ‚Äî Chainable HOC factory (.attrs(), .config())
- @pyreon/rocketstyle ‚Äî Multi-state styling (states, sizes, variants, themes, dark mode)
- @pyreon/coolgrid ‚Äî 12-column responsive grid (Container, Row, Col)
- @pyreon/kinetic ‚Äî CSS-transition animations (Transition, Stagger, Collapse)
- @pyreon/kinetic-presets ‚Äî 120+ animation presets
- @pyreon/connector-document ‚Äî Bridge to @pyreon/document
- @pyreon/document-primitives ‚Äî 18 rocketstyle document components ‚Äî render in browser AND export to 14+ formats via the same tree. `DocDocument` `title` / `author` / `subject` accept either strings or `() => string` accessors. Function values are stored in `_documentProps` and resolved by `extractDocumentTree` at extraction time, so each export click reads the LIVE value from any underlying signal ‚Äî no `const initial = get()` workaround needed.

```typescript
// PyreonUI ‚Äî unified provider (replaces 3 separate providers)
import { PyreonUI, useMode } from "@pyreon/ui-core"
import { enrichTheme } from "@pyreon/unistyle"

const theme = enrichTheme({ colors: { primary: "#3b82f6" } })

const App = () => (
  <PyreonUI theme={theme} mode="system">
    <MyApp />
  </PyreonUI>
)

// Read resolved mode anywhere:
const mode = useMode()  // "light" | "dark" (reactive signal)
```

## Ecosystem Packages (20)

- @pyreon/store ‚Äî Global state management ‚Äî Pinia-inspired composition stores returning StoreApi<T>. Stores are singletons by ID ‚Äî the setup function runs once. Calling the returned hook multiple times returns the same StoreApi instance. Use `resetStore(id)` or `resetAllStores()` to force re-creation.
- @pyreon/state-tree ‚Äî Structured reactive state tree ‚Äî composable models with snapshots, patches, and middleware. State mutations must go through actions ‚Äî direct `.set()` calls on state signals bypass middleware and patch recording. The model enforces this in dev mode.
- @pyreon/form ‚Äî Signal-based form management with fields, validation, arrays, and cross-field watchers. Fields validate on blur by default so users aren't scolded mid-keystroke. Use `validateOn: "change"` for instant feedback (often paired with `debounceMs: 300` to avoid thrashing async validators), or `validateOn: "submit"` for zero-feedback-until-submit forms. `showError` (from `useField`) gates on `touched`, so even with `validateOn: "change"` errors won't appear until the user has blurred at least once ‚Äî this is intentional.
- @pyreon/validation ‚Äî Schema adapters for Pyreon forms ‚Äî Zod, Valibot, ArkType. All three schema libraries are optional peer dependencies. Install only the one you use ‚Äî the adapters are tree-shaken per import path (`@pyreon/validation/zod`, `@pyreon/validation/valibot`, `@pyreon/validation/arktype`).
- @pyreon/query ‚Äî TanStack Query adapter with signal-driven results + WebSocket subscriptions + SSE (useSSE). `useQuery` / `useInfiniteQuery` / `useQueries` / `useSuspenseQuery` take options as a FUNCTION (not an object) so `queryKey` and other fields can read Pyreon signals. TanStack core uses an object; Pyreon wraps so changing a tracked signal re-runs the observer options and refetches automatically.
- @pyreon/table ‚Äî Pyreon adapter for TanStack Table ‚Äî reactive options, signal-driven state, flexRender. Options must be a FUNCTION `() => TableOptions<T>`, not a plain object. Signal reads inside the function are tracked reactively ‚Äî changing any tracked signal re-syncs the table automatically.
- @pyreon/virtual ‚Äî Pyreon adapter for TanStack Virtual ‚Äî element-scoped and window-scoped virtualization. Both hooks return reactive signals (`virtualItems()`, `totalSize()`, `isScrolling()`). Always read them inside reactive scopes (JSX thunks, effect, computed) so they update on scroll.
- @pyreon/i18n ‚Äî Reactive i18n with async namespace loading, plurals, interpolation. `@pyreon/i18n` includes JSX components (Trans, I18nProvider, useI18n) and depends on `@pyreon/core`. `@pyreon/i18n/core` is framework-agnostic with only `@pyreon/reactivity` as a dependency ‚Äî use it for backends and edge workers.
- @pyreon/feature ‚Äî Schema-driven CRUD primitives ‚Äî define once, get queries, forms, tables, and stores. defineFeature composes 5 packages internally (@pyreon/query, @pyreon/form, @pyreon/validation, @pyreon/store, @pyreon/table). All must be installed, and a QueryClient provider must be mounted in the component tree for the query hooks to work.
- @pyreon/charts ‚Äî Reactive ECharts bridge with lazy loading, auto-detection, typed options. ECharts imports `tslib` whose ESM `./modules/index.js` entry destructures named helpers from a `__toESM(require_tslib())` default ‚Äî the helpers live as top-level vars on the CJS factory, so the destructure reads `undefined` and the page throws `TypeError: Cannot destructure property "__extends"` the moment ECharts loads. Use `chartsViteAlias()` from `@pyreon/charts/vite` in your `vite.config.ts` (`resolve: { alias: { ...chartsViteAlias() } }`); it resolves `tslib` to the flat-ESM `tslib.es6.js` across install layouts. Browser tests use `tslibBrowserAlias()` from the shared test config. Tracking upstream: microsoft/tslib#189.
- @pyreon/storage ‚Äî Reactive client-side storage ‚Äî localStorage, sessionStorage, cookies, IndexedDB. Browser-backed hooks (`useStorage`, `useSessionStorage`, `useIndexedDB`) return the default value on the server. `useCookie` is SSR-readable via `setCookieSource()` which reads from the request headers.
- @pyreon/hotkeys ‚Äî Keyboard shortcut management ‚Äî scope-aware, modifier keys, conflict detection. By default, shortcuts do NOT fire when focused on form elements (input, textarea, select). Pass `enableOnFormElements: true` in options to override. Escape is a common candidate for this override.
- @pyreon/permissions ‚Äî Reactive permissions ‚Äî RBAC, ABAC, feature flags, subscription tiers. `admin.*` matches `admin.users` but NOT `admin.users.list` ‚Äî wildcards match exactly one segment.
- @pyreon/machine ‚Äî Reactive state machines ‚Äî constrained signals with type-safe transitions. Unlike XState, Pyreon machines have no built-in context. Use regular signals alongside the machine for associated data ‚Äî the machine handles the state transitions, signals handle the data.
- @pyreon/flow ‚Äî Reactive flow diagrams ‚Äî signal-native nodes, edges, pan/zoom, auto-layout via elkjs (peer: @pyreon/runtime-dom). LayoutOptions.direction / layerSpacing / edgeRouting apply to layered/tree only ‚Äî force/stress/radial/box/rectpacking silently ignore them. nodeSpacing is the only field respected by every algorithm. Dev-mode console.warn fires when an option is set on an algorithm that ignores it.
- @pyreon/code ‚Äî Reactive code editor ‚Äî CodeMirror 6 with signals, minimap, diff editor, lazy-loaded languages (peer: @pyreon/runtime-dom). `@pyreon/runtime-dom` is required in consumer apps because `<CodeEditor>` JSX emits `_tpl()` / `_bind()` calls.
- @pyreon/document ‚Äî Universal document rendering ‚Äî 18 primitives, 14+ output formats. Heavy format renderers are lazy-loaded: PDF (~3MB via pdfmake + bundled fonts), DOCX (~700KB via docx), XLSX (~1.1MB via exceljs), PPTX (~400KB via pptxgenjs). First render of each format triggers the dynamic import; subsequent renders are instant. The vendored architecture means apps download all renderer chunks during npm install (14MB total `lib/`), but consumer-side bundlers tree-shake to only ship the renderers an app actually invokes.
- @pyreon/rx ‚Äî Signal-aware reactive transforms ‚Äî filter, map, sortBy, groupBy, pipe, debounce, throttle, 37 functions. Detection is purely `typeof source === "function"` (see `isSignal` in `rx/src/types.ts`) ‚Äî there is NO `.subscribe` / value inspection. Any function (the signal, an accessor wrapper `() => items()`, a bound method) is treated as reactive and called inside a computed. The actual mistake is the opposite of what you might expect: passing a RESOLVED value (`items()`, an already-read array) takes the static path and never updates. Pass the signal, not its resolved value.
- @pyreon/toast ‚Äî Toast notifications ‚Äî toast(), toast.success/error/warning/info/loading, Toaster component, a11y (peer: @pyreon/runtime-dom). Unlike most Pyreon packages, toast uses a module-level signal store ‚Äî `toast()` works from event handlers, effects, or any non-component code. The `<Toaster />` component reads from this shared store.
- @pyreon/url-state ‚Äî URL-synced state ‚Äî useUrlState(key, default) or schema mode, auto type coercion, SSR-safe. Type coercion is based on the default value: `useUrlState("page", 1)` coerces `?page=2` to number `2`. `useUrlState("page", "1")` keeps it as string `"1"`. Always provide the right type as default.
- @pyreon/storybook ‚Äî Storybook renderer for Pyreon components
- @pyreon/mcp ‚Äî Model Context Protocol server ‚Äî discoverability map, live API lookup, validation, migration, anti-pattern catalog, changelog, test-environment audit. Each `createServer()` instance maintains its own cached context (routes, components, islands). The cache auto-resets when `process.cwd()` changes between tool invocations, so the same server can operate across multiple projects in one session.
- @pyreon/lint ‚Äî Pyreon-specific linter ‚Äî 67 rules across 13 categories, config files, watch mode, AST cache, CLI + LSP

## Zero (Full-Stack Framework)

- @pyreon/zero ‚Äî Full-stack meta-framework: fs-routing, SSR/SSG/ISR/SPA, API routes, server actions, adapters, i18n. mode: 'ssg' returns Plugin[] (the SSG plugin auto-attaches a companion `ssgPlugin()`); Vite's plugins array flattens nested arrays so `plugins: [pyreon(), zero()]` works as-is.

@pyreon/zero ‚Äî full-stack meta-framework built on Pyreon + Vite:
- Client-safe main entry: `@pyreon/zero` = client components only, `@pyreon/zero/server` = server-only
- File-system routing (src/routes/) with layouts, error boundaries, loading states, 404 pages
- SSR + SSG + ISR rendering modes ‚Äî `mode: "ssg"` auto-wires the build-time prerender pipeline (`closeBundle` ‚Üí SSR sub-build ‚Üí write `dist/<path>/index.html` per `ssg.paths` entry; auto-detects static paths from the route tree when paths are omitted)
- API routes (src/routes/api/) with typed handlers
- Image optimization ‚Äî responsive srcset, blur placeholders, WebP/AVIF, lazy loading
- Favicon generation ‚Äî single source SVG/PNG ‚Üí ICO, Apple touch, manifest, dark mode SVG
- Locale-aware favicons ‚Äî `faviconPlugin({ locales: { de: { source: "./icon-de.svg" } } })`
- OG image generation ‚Äî `ogImagePlugin({ templates, locales })`
- Reactive favicon switching ‚Äî dual light/dark PNG/ICO with theme-synced media attribute swap
- Meta component ‚Äî declarative OG (og:image:width/height, og:video, og:audio), noIndex, ogTemplate, favicon prop
- I18n routing ‚Äî locale detection (Accept-Language + cookie), prefix strategies, useLocale()
- Font optimization ‚Äî Google Fonts, local fonts, font-display, fallback metrics
- Theme ‚Äî dark/light toggle with FOUC prevention script
- Middleware composition ‚Äî compose(), shared context, CORS, cache, compression, rate limiting
- CSP middleware ‚Äî `cspMiddleware({ directives })` with `useNonce()` for inline scripts
- Env validation ‚Äî `validateEnv({ PORT: 3000, DEBUG: false, API_KEY: String })`, `publicEnv()`
- Request logging ‚Äî `loggerMiddleware()`
- AI integration ‚Äî `aiPlugin()` for llms.txt, JSON-LD inference, AI plugin manifest
- `useRequestLocals()` ‚Äî bridge middleware locals into component tree
- SEO ‚Äî sitemap.xml, robots.txt, JSON-LD helpers
- Dev error overlay with stack traces
- Adapters ‚Äî Node, Bun, static, Vercel, Cloudflare Pages, Netlify Functions
- Layouts receive the route content as `props.children` (zero's `createApp` passes `<RouterView />` as the layout's child); render `{props.children}` inside the layout ‚Äî do NOT render `<RouterView />` directly, or you'll mount it twice

## Key Ecosystem Patterns

```typescript
// State management
import { defineStore } from '@pyreon/store'
const useCounter = defineStore('counter', () => {
  const count = signal(0)
  const increment = () => count.update(n => n + 1)
  return { count, increment }
})

// Forms
import { useForm, useField } from '@pyreon/form'
const form = useForm({ initialValues: { name: '' }, onSubmit: (v) => save(v) })
const name = useField(form, 'name')

// Permissions
import { createPermissions } from '@pyreon/permissions'
const can = createPermissions({ 'posts.read': true, 'posts.delete': (post) => post.authorId === userId })

// State machines
import { createMachine } from '@pyreon/machine'
const light = createMachine({ initial: 'red', states: { red: { on: { NEXT: 'green' } }, green: { on: { NEXT: 'yellow' } }, yellow: { on: { NEXT: 'red' } } } })

// Document rendering
import { createDocument } from '@pyreon/document'
const doc = createDocument({ title: 'Report' }).heading('Sales').table({ columns: ['Region', 'Revenue'], rows: [['US', '$1M']] })
await doc.toPdf()   // PDF
await doc.toEmail() // Outlook-safe HTML
await doc.toSlack() // Slack Block Kit JSON

// Reactive transforms
import { filter, pipe, sortBy } from '@pyreon/rx'
const items = signal([{ name: 'B', age: 30 }, { name: 'A', age: 25 }])
const adults = filter(items, i => i.age >= 18)  // Computed<Item[]>
const sorted = pipe(items, sortBy('name'), filter(i => i.age > 20))

// Toast notifications
import { toast, Toaster } from '@pyreon/toast'
toast.success('Saved!')
toast.promise(saveData(), { loading: 'Saving...', success: 'Done!', error: 'Failed' })

// URL-synced state
import { useUrlState } from '@pyreon/url-state'
const page = useUrlState('page', 1)     // synced to ?page=
const filters = useUrlState({ page: 1, sort: 'name' })  // schema mode

// SSE subscription
import { useSSE } from '@pyreon/query'
const { data } = useSSE({ queryKey: ['events'], url: '/api/events' })

// Active route detection
import { useIsActive } from '@pyreon/router'
const isAdmin = useIsActive('/admin')  // reactive boolean

// Typed search params with auto-coercion
import { useTypedSearchParams } from '@pyreon/router'
const params = useTypedSearchParams({ page: 'number', q: 'string' })
params.page()  // number

// Route transition state
import { useTransition } from '@pyreon/router'
const { isTransitioning } = useTransition()

// View Transitions API ‚Äî auto-enabled, opt out per route:
{ path: '/modal', component: Modal, meta: { viewTransition: false } }
// `await router.push()` / `.replace()` resolves AFTER the DOM swap (vt.updateCallbackDone),
// NOT after the full animation (.finished, 200-300ms). afterEach hooks + scrollManager.restore
// also fire post-commit ‚Äî they see the NEW route state.

// Middleware chain with data passing
const auth: RouteMiddleware = async (ctx) => { ctx.data.user = await getUser(ctx.request) }
// In component: const data = useMiddlewareData<{ user: User }>()
```

## Documentation

Full docs at `docs/` (VitePress site, 52 pages): `cd docs && bun run dev`

## AI Tools

- `pyreon doctor --json` ‚Äî scan project for React patterns, get structured fix suggestions
- `pyreon doctor --fix` ‚Äî auto-fix React patterns to Pyreon equivalents
- `pyreon context` ‚Äî generate .pyreon/context.json with routes, components, islands
- MCP server: `bunx @pyreon/mcp` ‚Äî live API lookup, validation, migration, diagnostics

## Dev-mode warnings ‚Äî bundler tree-shake

Pyreon dev warnings are gated on `import.meta.env?.DEV`. Source pattern √ó bundler tree-shake:

- inline `if (!import.meta.env?.DEV) return` ‚Äî tree-shaken in both Vite and raw esbuild.
- `const __DEV__ = ...; if (__DEV__) ...` ‚Äî tree-shaken in Vite, mostly tree-shaken in raw esbuild.
- `const __DEV__ = ...; __DEV__ && cond && warn(...)` (chained &&) ‚Äî tree-shaken in Vite, runtime-gated only in raw esbuild (string survives in bundle but warn doesn't fire because gate is false).
- `typeof process !== 'undefined'` ‚Äî dead code in all browser bundlers; forbidden by `pyreon/no-process-dev-gate` lint rule.

Vite is Pyreon's primary supported bundler. Non-Vite consumers using the chained form pay a small bundle-size cost only ‚Äî runtime is correct. Tests live in `runtime-dom/src/tests/dev-gate-treeshake.test.ts` (Vite + non-Vite runtime smoke) and `flow/src/tests/integration.test.ts` (esbuild for the inline pattern).
